data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Tesla, Apple and Boeing stocks among top picks
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday ended lower on Friday, after a hotter-than-expected jobs report dampened hopes for more interest rate cuts. For the week, the S&P (SP500) fell nearly 1%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) lost 0.2%. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) declined 1.9%

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Tesla downgraded by BofA on execution risk

Bank of America lowered its rating on Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) to Neutral from Buy, citing high valuation and near-term execution risk.

"While this still implies upside, execution risk is high and TSLA is trading at a level that captures much of our base case LT potential from core autos, robotaxi, Optimus, and energy generation & storage," warned analyst John Murphy, adding that the brokerage is also seeing some risk of new government policies being less favorable for Tesla than expected.

Apple downgraded on high valuation concern

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) was in focus as the tech giant was downgraded by MoffettNathanson to Sell from Neutral amid concerns about its high valuation.

"The outlook for Apple’s shares, given this challenging backdrop, is, unfortunately, decidedly unattractive, in our view," Moffett added, and put a PT of $188 on the stock.

AT&T upgraded on growth initiatives by RBC, T-Mobile receives rating cut

RBC upgraded AT&T (NYSE:T) to Outperform from Sector Perform, saying that it has confidence in the telecom giant’s growth initiatives, which stand to benefit from subscriber growth anchored by fiber investments going ahead. The brokerage also hiked PT by $4 to $26.

Meanwhile, T-Mobile US (NASDAQ:TMUS) was downgraded to Sector Perform from Outperform. RBC said though execution at the company appears solid, the analysts see less favorable reward/risk based on current valuation. The PT was trimmed by $15 to $240.

Barclays upgrades Boeing on higher plane output

Boeing (NYSE:BA) was upgraded to Overweight from Equal Weight by analysts by Barclays, with analysts saying that the aviation and defense company is poised to gain as its output of planes, especially the best-selling 737 Max, drives higher free cash flow.

Barclays, which raised its PT on the stock to $210 from $190, said Boeing shored up its balance sheet, and it's no longer an overhang, while it believes that new outside leadership can affect meaningful change that's necessary.

Shell upgraded by UBS on hefty free cash flow

Shell (NYSE:SHEL) was upgraded by UBS to Buy from Neutral, even after the company’s disappointing Q4 update. The brokerage noted Shell’s hefty free cash flow and ~14% free cash flow yield that ranks highest among European oil stocks.

UBS analyst Joshua Stone sees an opportunity for $6B of additional cost savings, with the company on track to meet or exceed its 2025 targets.

Analysts bullish on BofA, Citigroup; GS receives downgrade

HSBC and UBS upgraded Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) to Buy, citing the higher-for-longer rate environment as benefiting the money center bank. Truist also initiated coverage of BofA with a Buy rating.

Citigroup (NYSE:C) was also upgraded by Barclays to Overweight from Equalweight, as analyst Jason Goldberg expects accelerated earnings growth, higher investment banking fees, strong operating leverage and greater capital return, among others.

Meanwhile, UBS downgraded Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) to Neutral from Buy as the firm's business model relies heavily on Global Banking & Markets, and a "considerable" capital markets recovery is already priced into the stock's valuation.

HSBC downgraded AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) to Reduce from Buy, citing the belief of a "less competitive" offering compared to what Nvidia has. Analyst Frank Lee, who also reduced PT to $110 from $200, said AMD is unlikely to "penetrate" the AI GPU market as much as previously thought.

Stifel said Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) remains its "best idea" for artificial intelligence and accelerated computing in 2025. The brokerage thinks that the company will likely maintain its strong leadership position in the AI infrastructure market as its Blackwell architecture ramp begins in earnest in 2025.

Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) was downgraded by Deutsche Bank and BTIG to Hold from Buy and cut its price target to $190 from $207 amid an expected slowdown in security spending this year.

data.title: Wall Street's tough week pushes S&P 500 into negative territory for the new year
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) on Friday slipped 1.94% for the holiday-shortened week to end at 5,827.04 points, posting losses in two out of four sessions. Its accompanying SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY) also fell 1.94% for the week.

It was a sobering week for Wall Street, which left the benchmark index negative for the new year. The S&P (SP500) is now -4.48% from its record intraday high of 6,099.97 points. In the six trading days of 2025 so far, the gauge has ended in the red three times, including two drops of more than 1%.

A flood of economic data on the labor market and inflation this week indicated high resilience in the former and concerning signals regarding the latter. In response, market participants rushed to recalibrate their Federal Reserve interest rate cut bets.

Several central bank policymakers also hinted that monetary policy easing might be put on hold. Governor Michelle Bowman event went on to say that she "could have supported taking no action at the December (monetary policy committee) meeting," citing a "lack of continued progress on lowering inflation and the ongoing strength in economic activity and in the labor market."

According to the CME FedWatch tool, traders are now pricing in only two quarter-point interest rate cuts in 2025. Major brokerages also dialed back their expectations, with JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs also predicting just two rate cuts this year. Bank of America one-upped them and said it sees no more rate cuts at all, adding that there appeared to be greater risks toward policymakers now raising rates.

The first jolt this week came on Tuesday, ironically in the form of the November Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey (JOLTS). Job openings surged, while the quits rate edged down. Following a market holiday on Thursday for a national day of mourning for former U.S. President Jimmy Carter, investors returned on Friday with their sights squarely set on the December nonfarm payrolls report.

The data showed a whopping 256K jobs added to the U.S. economy, easily outstripping the consensus of +157K. Meanwhile, the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.1%, coming in lower than the expected figure of 4.2%.

Both indicators suggest that labor market has once again become highly resilient after showing considerable cooling in the middle of last year.

Meanwhile, in terms of inflation, prices paid by services organizations for materials and services increased to a nearly two-year high in December. Meanwhile, a gauge of long-run inflation expectations among consumers soared to its highest level since 2008.

Wall Street's focus now shifts to the earnings season, which will kick off in earnest in the second half of January. The political landscape is also very much on investors' minds, with incoming U.S. President Donald Trump's inauguration fast approaching.

"The weight of the evidence in our work suggests we are seeing a reset of market prices and sentiment, which had become stretched on a short-term basis, though it is still within the confines of an ongoing bull market," Keith Lerner, co-chief investment officer at Truist, said on Friday.

"When expectations are high, a little bad news can go a long way. And the proximate cause of the recent market setback has been the sharp move higher in interest rates. Our long-held view remains that we would prefer a stronger economy with fewer rate cuts than a weaker economy that requires more aggressive rate cuts," Lerner added.

Turning to the weekly performance of the S&P 500 (SP500) sectors, eight of the 11 ended in negative territory. Real Estate and Technology led the losers. Energy, Health Care and Materials were the three winners. See below a breakdown of the performance of the sectors as well as their accompanying SPDR Select Sector ETFs from January 3 close to January 10 close:

#1: Energy +0.90%, and the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLE) +1.05%.

#2: Health Care +0.52%, and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLV) +0.49%.

#3: Materials +0.14%, and the Materials Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLB) +0.13%.

#4: Communication Services -0.70%, and the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC) -2.11%.

#5: Industrials -1.01%, and the Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLI) -1.05%.

#6: Consumer Staples -1.92%, and the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLP) -2.54%.

#7: Utilities -1.96%, and the Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLU) -1.95%.

#8: Consumer Discretionary -2.34%, and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLY) -2.24%.

#9: Financials -2.71%, and the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLF) -2.61%.

#10: Information Technology -3.10%, and the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLK) -2.96%.

#11: Real Estate -4.10%, and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLRE) -4.06%.

For investors looking to track the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500), here are some exchange-traded funds of interest: (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

For investors looking into the future of what's happening, take a look at the Seeking Alpha Catalyst Watch to see next week's breakdown of actionable events that stand out.

data.title: Here’s the ‘secret sauce’ for S&P to notch another double-digit gain
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) in 2024 logged a second straight annual return of +20%, and how bond yields travel in 2025 will play an important role in the benchmark's yearly performance, Bank of America said.

The benchmark (SP500)"will likely have another big double-digit move," BofA said in seeking a history lesson into what comes next after two consecutive annual returns of +20%. In its Flow Show note on Friday, it found such baseline moves in 1927/28, 1935/36, 1954/55 and 1995/96 over the past 150 years.

"Falling bond yields = secret sauce for SPX to avoid huge reversals," of double-digit increases, and to "catalyze further big equity gains, as occurred in 1997/98," BofA Investment Strategist Michael Hartnett said.

In 2024, the S&P 500 (SP500) advanced +23%, following its +24% jump in 2023. Here's BofA's table:

Treasury yields (US10Y)(US2Y)(US30Y) have spiked up partially as the Federal Reserve -- still dealing with above-target inflation -- last month signaled two rate cuts in 2025 compared with its previous flag of four reductions.

See how yields are trading across the entire curve on Seeking Alpha’s bond page.

A second inflation round that forces the Fed to raise rates and sends Treasury yields surging to 6% would derail the U.S. exceptionalism view held by global fund managers, who, in December, were at a record overweight in U.S. stocks, Hartnett said.

Soaring yields could push consumer financial delinquencies or a recession and a liquidation of stocks and cryptocurrencies, he said. "Best play" in such a scenario is gold (XAUUSD:CUR), which could see its price driving up to $3,333, the strategist said. Gold traded above $2,685 on Friday.

The 10-year yield (US10Y) on Friday hit 4.79% and the 30-year yield (US30Y) reached 5% after a hot December jobs report sparked recalibration in rate-cut bets. BofA economists on Friday said they anticipate no Fed rate cuts this year.

Here are some ETFs that track the S&P 500 (SP500): (SPY), (SPXU), (VOO), (IVV), and (UPRO).

data.title: Sage confirms Biogen’s bid to acquire remaining stake
data.description:
Sage Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SAGE) has confirmed that the Alzheimer’s drug developer Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB) has offered to buy all the outstanding shares of common stock that it currently does not own for $7.22 apiece.

The Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotech added that its board of directors, along with its independent financial and legal advisors, will evaluate Biogen’s (NASDAQ:BIIB) bid and decide on the next steps.

The offer is “unsolicited and nonbinding,” and “there is no guarantee that any transaction will result from Biogen’s proposal,” SAGE said in a regulatory filing late Friday. However, its shares surged in the post-market after the announcement.

Following a collaboration agreement in 2020 to develop multiple therapeutic candidates, Biogen (BIIB) and Sage (NASDAQ:SAGE) market Zurzuvae (zuranolone), a treatment indicated for women with postpartum depression ((PPD).

Biogen (BIIB) currently owns a 10.2% stake in SAGE, and its offer price implies a ~30% premium to the company’s closing price on Friday.

data.title: President Biden criticizes Meta's plan to end fact-checking
data.description:
President Joe Biden on Friday criticized Meta Platforms' (NASDAQ:META) move to end third-party fact checking on its social media platforms in the U.S., in lieu of a community notes system similar to Elon Musk's X.

"It’s just completely contrary to everything America is about. We want to tell the truth. We haven’t always done it as a nation," said Biden in a press conference.

The social media giants announced major changes this week. Meta (META) said it will replace its fact-checking program in the U.S. with a community notes system and simplify its content policies and eliminate restrictions on topics like immigration and gender.

Policy enforcement will change as well, such as refocusing screening filters to tackle illegal and high-severity violations, while lower violations will rely on user reporting before the company takes action. The company's moderation and safety teams will be moved out of California, with U.S. content review to be based in Texas.

Meta (META) has made several moves in recent months as U.S. president-elect Donald Trump prepares to assume office for a second time. The company has gone from banning Trump on its platform in the aftermath of January 6 to Zuckerberg flying down to Mar-a-Lago in November to meet with him.

The company has appointed UFC chief executive Dana White, a vocal Trump supporter, to its board and is even donating $1 million to Trump's inaugural fund.

"In recent years we’ve developed increasingly complex systems to manage content across our platforms, partly in response to societal and political pressure to moderate content," said Joel Kaplan, Meta’s (META) chief global affairs officer. "This approach has gone too far."

Trump, who has railed against social media companies' online moderation efforts in the past, hailed the move and said that Meta (META) had "come a long way."

Meta (META) did not immediately respond to a Seeking Alpha request for comment.

data.title: Elon Musk expects 20-30 patients to receive upgraded Neuralink brain implants this year
data.description:
Billionaire investor Elon Musk says 20-30 patients are expected to receive upgraded versions of an experimental brain-computer implant developed by his neurotechnology company, Neuralink, this year.

“We’ve got three humans with Neuralink implanted, now working well,” Musk, the CEO of electric vehicle maker Tesla (TSLA) and space exploration company SpaceX (SPACE), said during an event streamed on his social media platform X this week.

Neuralink’s technology, known as a brain-computer interface, consists of a skull-mounted, wireless implant connected to the brain via electrode threads.

The rechargeable device is designed to help patients with neurological conditions such as limb paralysis and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) control computers and other external devices only with their thoughts.

“We have upgraded the device so that the devices will have more electrodes, basically higher bandwidth, and longer battery life,” Musk added. “So we expect to hopefully do 20 or 30 patients this year with the upgraded Neuralink devices."

Since receiving the FDA clearance to start human trials in 2023, Neuralink is currently running two U.S.-based trials, PRIME and CONVOY, to evaluate BCI.

In January 2024, Noland Arbaugh, a 30-year-old accident victim with paraplegia, became the first patient to receive the implant as part of the company’s PRIME study. In August, Neuralink announced the successful implantation of the second patient with the device.

data.title: CES focused on AI and accelerated compute. These companies are likely to benefit.
data.description:
The 2025 edition of CES had its usual announcements related to automobiles, robot vacuums and more. But like last year, the overriding theme was artificial intelligence and accelerated computing. And while everyone wants a piece of the AI pie, J.P. Morgan expects only a handful of companies, including Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), to outperform based on product strength.

"In AI/accelerated compute, Nvidia continues to see a strong demand setup for CY25 as it brings to market its Blackwell platform (ramp is tracking in-line with expectations)," analysts at the firm wrote in a note. "Longer term, the team views AI/accelerated compute as a multi-year infrastructure build out as AI accelerators captures more share of the $1T data center infrastructure installed base."

Conversely, companies exposed to consumer facing markets — like smartphones and internet of things — are seeing demand "tracking in-line with expectations," while the automotive and industrial parts of the market are still mixed, the analysts added.

Marvell

Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) executives believe the company's AI-related revenue is tracking "significantly ahead" of its previous expectations for $2.5B in 2025, with 2026 likely to grow as well, amid strong demand for optical and application specific integrated circuits.

Management also said the revenue target of $8B by 2028 for custom ASICs may now be "conservative," aiding other companies like Snyopsys (SNPS) (chip/system design activity) and Lam Research (LRCX) (leading-edge equipment tools). Marvell's custom ASIC business is driven by Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), but Microsoft (MSFT) is likely to further fuel growth, the analysts added.

Additionally, Marvell has expanded into other areas, such as scale-up networking applications (in-rack connectivity), co-packaged optics, active electrical cables and linear pluggable optics, all of which could drive further growth.

Nvidia

Nvidia is likely to keep benefiting, as spending on AI and accelerated computing remain "strong" over the next decade and beyond, Nvidia CFO Colette Kress told J.P. Morgan's analysts.

Strong data center momentum should continue in 2025, amid the ramp up and demand for Nvidia's line of Blackwell GPUs, now in full production.

"In the longer term, the team expects significant revenue growth opportunities as it captures a larger percentage of the $1 trillion in datacenter infrastructure installed base," the analysts wrote. "This growth will be driven by a shift to accelerated computing and more demand for AI solutions."

Additionally, the analysts said Nvidia believes it has a "strong competitive advantage" against custom ASICs, due to its full software stack, comprehensive offerings, ecosystem and ease of adoption.

And while the company may not have said anything about its upcoming Rubin line of GPUs, it did launch several products geared towards the gaming product and a small supercomputer, Project Digits, geared towards AI projects.

"...[We] believe Nvidia is in a unique position to expand its installed base beyond high-end gaming and into the consumer market driven by proliferation of AI at the edge and its installed base of RTX PC platforms," J.P. Morgan analysts explained.

Skyworks Solutions

Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) is expecting a normal, seasonal demand trend in its business.

"Overall, we believe the team is on track with the seasonal shipment profile for the December quarter as highlighted during the last earnings call," J.P. Morgan analysts wrote. "The team also believes that iPhone 16 demand trends are aligning with expectations. Looking ahead, we anticipate normal seasonality in the business for the March quarter (typically a 12-14% Q/Q decline) and the June quarter (flat to down Q/Q)."

Additionally, Apple (AAPL) may launch an iPhone SE model early in 2025, which is expected to be "small" in volume and should not impact any seasonal trends, the analysts added. And with Apple focusing on an internal modem for the iPhone, Skyworks could see a return to its historical growth of around 10% content growth in radio frequency in the smartphone, J.P. Morgan analysts added.

There are also some "green shoots" in broad markets, except for industrial and infrastructure networking, the analysts added. And with Skyworks continuing to generate strong free cash flow, there is room to repurchase around $1B in stock annually, the analysts explained.

Micron

Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) expects to see a strong second-half of 2025 as AI is likely to keep driving the demand for high-bandwidth memory, the analysts said. Additionally, the non-AI part of Micron's business likely to see improvement in supply and demand, they added.

"The team struck an overall positive tone on data center demand with DRAM/NAND bit shipments expected to return to Q/Q growth in the May-Qtr on the back of strong AI-driven HBM/eSSD demand, overlaid with cyclical recovery in PC/smartphone segments as healthier customer inventory levels and increased adoption of edge AI devices drive renewed memory procurement given higher memory content requirements (we believe 40-60% higher memory content)," the analysts wrote.

They continued: "Furthermore, the team is seeing strong demand for its high capacity DRAM products (HBM, high capacity DIMMS and LP5x-based server DRAM) and remains confident that these products will generate multiple billions of dollars of revenue in FY25."

data.title: Apple Intelligence has failed to boost iPhone sales: Ming-Chi Kuo
data.description:
Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) much hyped artificial intelligence enhancements have failed to boost sluggish iPhone sales, said analyst Ming-Chi Kuo on Friday, adding that the company's users just aren't interested in Apple Intelligence.

The Cupertino giant unveiled its AI features with much fanfare in July, but only started rolling them out in December in phases. While it released features like integrating OpenAI's ChatGPT and Image Playgrounds with iOS 18.2, the more advanced Siri with on-screen awareness is scheduled for launch in April.

In recent years, the company has drawn criticism for being slow to announce its AI strategy, while rivals Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN) and Meta (META) pulled ahead in the emerging field.

Despite the initial buzz following Apple Intelligence’s debut at WWDC 2024, "its appeal has significantly declined compared to cloud-based AI services, which have advanced rapidly in subsequent months," said Kuo in a blog post.

Some analysts have predicted users upgrading their hardware, prompted by Apple Intelligence. Investment firm Wedbush believes Apple (AAPL) could sell over 240 million iPhone units in fiscal year 2025 as the AI-driven upgrade cycle takes hold, which would make it the highest iPhone unit sales year in the company's history.

A SellCell survey last month revealed that most users believed that AI features added little to no value.

Analysts have also been hopeful that Apple Intelligence could create a new services revenue stream through subscriptions. In an interview with Wired last month, chief executive Tim Cook said that the company had never internally discussed charging for Apple Intelligence.

"I’m not bearish on Apple Intelligence’s long-term prospects; however, given the points discussed above, there is no evidence of Apple Intelligence’s ability to benefit hardware replacement cycles or service business," said Kuo.

data.title: Crude oil spikes most in three months as new Russia oil sanctions 'taken more seriously'
data.description:
Crude oil futures rallied to fresh three-month highs Friday, after the Biden administration broadened sanctions on Russian oil, potentially affecting global supplies, while winter weather lifts demand for heating fuels and threatens production freeze-offs.

The U.S. Treasury Department announced sanctions targeting two major Russian oil producers - Gazprom Neft and Surgutneftegas - and imposed sanctions on several oil-carrying vessels, Russia-based oilfield service providers and Russian energy officials.

Sources in Russian oil trade and Indian refining said the sanctions will severely disrupt Russian oil exports to its major buyers India and China and make them more expensive, Reuters reported.

The timing just days before President-elect Trump's inauguration makes it likely that Trump will keep the sanctions in place and use them as a negotiating tool for a Ukraine peace treaty, UBS analyst Giovanni Staunovo said.

"This is definitely upping the game when it comes to enforcement of Russian sanctions," Phil Flynn of Price Futures Group said, adding that the the world had somewhat "turned a blind eye" to Russia's efforts to sidestep sanctions on its oil sector because the world needed its oil.

Oil prices also were helped as extreme cold in the U.S. and Europe boosted demand for heating oil; front-month February U.S. ultra-low sulfur diesel futures climbed another 5.2% on Friday to settle at more than $2.50/gal, the highest since July 15.

The sanctions moves "are being taken more seriously now because we’re starting to see China and India back away from actually buying the sanctioned barrels,” Rebecca Babin of CIBC Private Wealth US said, adding that previous buyer reaction to increased sanctions was “yeah, whatever, you’re not going to enforce, [but] now they’re taking it a bit more seriously.”

But the reaction to the sanctions and the weather is “somewhat temporary,” Babin also said, believing “rallies above $80 Brent are going to get sold eventually, because of the spare capacity that’s out there, and we know that if we keep going higher OPEC is going to come back with production.”

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for February delivery settled +3.6% on Friday, its biggest daily gain since October 7, to $76.57/bbl, and front-month March Brent crude (CO1:COM) closed +3.7% on Friday to $79.76/bbl, topping $80 intraday for the first time since October; for the week, the benchmarks gained 3.5% and 4.2%, respectively.

U.S. natural gas futures rose for a third straight session, as forecasts turn even colder for the coming weeks and moderate freeze-offs keep a lid on production; the Nymex February front-month contract (NG1:COM) ended +7.8% on Friday at $3.989/MMBtu for an 18.9% weekly gain.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (UNG), (BIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Europe is bracing for the coldest weather in four years, spreading from the U.K. and parts of Scandanavia to the continent, further pressuring gas inventories as heating demand increases.

The frigid temperatures are pushing up heating demand to its highest level this winter, days after Russian gas flows were halted through a key pipeline route via Ukraine; Europe’s gas storages are now depleting at the fastest rate in eight years.

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLE), ended the week +2%.

Top 20 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Brenmiller Energy (BNRG) +91.1%, Nouveau Monde Graphite (NMG) +28.9%, Constellation Energy (CEG) +25.8%, Plug Power (PLUG) +23.2%, MP Materials (MP) +20.5%, Critical Minerals (CRML) +18.6%, Frontline (FRO) +17.3%, Lightbridge (LTBR) +17.2%, Kolibri Global Energy (KGEI) +16.9%, Clean Energy Fuels (CLNE) +15.6%, Oklo (OKLO) +15.5%, Top Ships (TOPS) +14.9%, Equinox Gold (EQX) +14.9%, Nuscale Power (SMG) +13.6%, Vizsla Silver (VZLA) +13.5%, Nordic American Tanker (NAT) +13.3%, North European Oil Royalty Trust (NRT) +13.3%, Compass Minerals (CMP) +12.8%, Vital Energy (VTLE) +12.2%, International Seaways (INSW) +12.2%.

Top 10 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Zeo Energy (ZEO) -40.5%, Maxeon Solar (MAXN) -24%, Empresa Distribuidora y Comercializadora (EDN) -21.4%, CNX Resources (CNX) -19.6%, Edison International (EIX) -18.7%, Zim Integrated Shipping (ZIM) -18.1%, Eco Wave Power (WAVE) -17.4%, FTC Solar (FTCI) -16.6%, Ramaco Resources (METC) -16%, Ascent Solar Technologies (ASTI) -15.5%.

Source: Barchart.com

data.title: Traws stock rallies 24% on avian flu drug update
data.description:
Shares of Traws Pharma (NASDAQ:TRAW) shot up 24% in post-market trading Friday on encouraging results from a Phase 1 study of the company's drug Tivoxavir Marboxil in the treatment of H5N1 avian flu.

The study showed a single dose of the drug maintained plasma drug levels above the effective concentration, EC90, for over 23 days, indicating the potential for sustained efficacy with a one-time administration. No treatment-related adverse events were reported, according to a statement.

The company said plans were underway to initiate a Phase 2 study of the product in the first half of 2025.
